mappings.rationaleAI: Directly covers photography as an art, including major photographers (Adams, Cartier-Bresson, Capa), photojournalism, and the nature of photography (D).
Covers the history of Western drawing and painting with references to artists and techniques like camera obscura, relevant to A.4, B.6.a, C.4.
Addresses aesthetics of visual arts including representation, significant form, and taste -- central to design elements and iconography (A.1, B.1, B.2).
Covers modern painting movements including Cubism (Braque), abstraction (Mondrian), and nonrepresentational painting (B.5.b.ii, B.6.a).
Covers contemporary painting, drawing, and photography practices including the global art market and biennials (B.6.a, D).
Renaissance masters (da Vinci, Durer, van Eyck) are central to Western painting and drawing history, covering techniques and manuscript illumination (A.4, B.5.a.ii, B.6.a).
Dada and Surrealist painting, collage, and printmaking (De Chirico, Magritte, Miro) expanded artistic techniques in Western painting history (B.6.a).
Avant-garde movements transformed painting from Impressionism (Seurat) through Cubism (Picasso), relevant to Western painting history (B.6.a).
Egyptian mural painting and drawing traditions are relevant to painting on fixed objects (B.5.a.i) and non-Western painting (B.6.b).
Christian painting traditions including devotional painting, manuscript illumination, and stained glass are directly relevant to B.4.f, B.5.a.ii, B.5.b.i.
Covers letterforms, fonts, and printing design, directly relevant to printmaking media and the relationship between type and graphic arts (C).
Futurist painting is a significant movement in Western painting history, notable for depicting motion and modern life (B.6.a).
Prehistoric cave painting at sites like Lascaux is the earliest form of painting on fixed objects (B.5.a.i) and primitive painting (B.5.c).
Romantic painting including landscape painting is a major period in Western painting history (B.5.b.i, B.6.a).
Covers calligraphy and writing systems, directly relevant to the relationship between drawing and calligraphy (B.4.a) referenced in this section.
Covers photojournalism and press photography, directly relevant to kinds of functional photography (D.3.b).
